content.problemStatement:
Chef has three empty stacks and NNN numbers from 111 to NNN.

Each number is randomly placed on **one** of the stacks with equal probability of 13\frac{1}{3}31​ for each stack.

The numbers are placed in ascending order, meaning that if x<yx \lt yx<y, then xxx must be placed on a stack before yyy is placed.

Find the probability that the **sum** of the **top** elements in the first **two** stacks is equal to MMM.

The given probability can be represented as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998244353998244353998244353, where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Note that you might not place anything on some stack and if that stack is one of the first two, it will contribute 000 to the sum.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM, where NNN is the number of integers and MMM is the sum desired from the top elements of first two stacks.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the probability that the **sum** of the **top** elements in the first **two** stacks is equal to MMM.


The given probability can be represented as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998244353998244353998244353, where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
